beta_diversity

Soroush_Torfehnejad

2026-03-29

Step 1)

Load libraries and Data, I rarefied the data based on the conclusions we came to when looking at the QUIME results.The rarefaction number is 5396

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
## Loading required package: permute
# Load object
ps <- readRDS('../processed_data/phyloseq_taxonomy.rds')


# rarefy the data:
psrare <- ps|>
          rarefy_even_depth(sample.size = 5396, rngseed = 421)
## `set.seed(421)` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling.
## Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.
## Try `set.seed(421); .Random.seed` for the full vector
## ...
## 4 samples removedbecause they contained fewer reads than `sample.size`.
## Up to first five removed samples are: 
## 
## SRR5275080SRR5275088SRR5275119SRR5275137
## ...
## 233OTUs were removed because they are no longer 
## present in any sample after random subsampling
## 
## ...
table(sample_sums(psrare))
## 
## 5396 
##  111

#Step 2) ### Caclculate the beta diversity using the Bray-Curtis distance matrix.

# Calculate the distance between each sample pair
ps_WUF <- phyloseq::distance(psrare, method = "wunifrac")
View(as.matrix(ps_WUF)) # Every pairwise comparison included

#Step 3) ### use Multidimensional scaling to reduce the distance matrix into fewer dimentions which will help plot abd understand the Data.

# MDS scaling
set.seed(421)
mds = metaMDS(ps_WUF)
## Run 0 stress 0.1201624 
## Run 1 stress 0.1212129 
## Run 2 stress 0.1239878 
## Run 3 stress 0.1241051 
## Run 4 stress 0.1240037 
## Run 5 stress 0.1211439 
## Run 6 stress 0.1207586 
## Run 7 stress 0.1212838 
## Run 8 stress 0.122485 
## Run 9 stress 0.1201606 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.02317015  max resid 0.1220737 
## Run 10 stress 0.1211576 
## Run 11 stress 0.1223413 
## Run 12 stress 0.1206996 
## Run 13 stress 0.1210861 
## Run 14 stress 0.123433 
## Run 15 stress 0.1208589 
## Run 16 stress 0.1209765 
## Run 17 stress 0.1204529 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.02536997  max resid 0.1198687 
## Run 18 stress 0.1241728 
## Run 19 stress 0.1230333 
## Run 20 stress 0.1250408 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     13: no. of iterations >= maxit
##      7: stress ratio > sratmax
# Extract data
mds_data = mds$points %>% as.data.frame %>%
  merge(sample_data(psrare), by='row.names', sort=F)
head(mds_data)
##    Row.names         MDS1          MDS2  sample_identifier num_bases
## 1 SRR5275058 -0.001806553  0.0090676991 UCD.13.01486.PA.H4  14670000
## 2 SRR5275059  0.038767194 -0.0054252048 UCD.13.01483.VB.H4  19774500
## 3 SRR5275060  0.009382436  0.0003721992 UCD.13.01399.VB.H5  18731500
## 4 SRR5275061  0.018850678 -0.0070993412 UCD.13.01398.VB.H4  17838000
## 5 SRR5275062  0.014565360 -0.0039544314 UCD.13.01397.VB.H5  12170000
## 6 SRR5275063  0.015568747 -0.0057154278 UCD.13.01396.VB.H4  19480000
##   collection_date country      state         county
## 1      2013-07-30     USA California  Solano County
## 2      2013-07-30     USA California  Solano County
## 3      2013-07-23     USA California  Solano County
## 4      2013-07-23     USA California  Solano County
## 5      2013-07-23     USA California  Solano County
## 6      2013-07-23     USA California  Solano County
##               precise_location       region influenza_status    sex
## 1 Grizzly Island Wildlife Area Suisun Marsh              Pos   male
## 2 Grizzly Island Wildlife Area Suisun Marsh              Pos   male
## 3 Grizzly Island Wildlife Area Suisun Marsh              Pos female
## 4 Grizzly Island Wildlife Area Suisun Marsh              Pos female
## 5 Grizzly Island Wildlife Area Suisun Marsh              Pos   male
## 6 Grizzly Island Wildlife Area Suisun Marsh              Pos female
##   influenza_level ct_value genotype ha_subtype na_subtype HA_NA ha_aggr na_aggr
## 1               2    39.82       21          4          6  H4N6       4       6
## 2               3    30.94       21          4          6  H4N6       4       6
## 3               3    32.48       28          5          5  H5N5       5       5
## 4               2    36.17       21          4          6  H4N6       4       6
## 5               3    31.35       28          5          5  H5N5       5       5
## 6               2    38.17       21          4          6  H4N6       4       6
##   ha_group
## 1        2
## 2        2
## 3        1
## 4        2
## 5        1
## 6        2

#Step 4) ### Plot the data based on the following comparisons, different HA/NA subtypes, different HA subtypes, different NA subtypes, finally Infected Vs non infection comparison.

# looking at Beta diversity differences between HA group 1, group 2 and negative control

plot_HA_G1_VS_G2_VS_neg<- mds_data %>%
                  ggplot(aes(MDS1,MDS2,color = ha_group)) +
                  geom_point() +
                  stat_ellipse() +
                  theme_classic(base_size=18)



# looking at Beta diversity differences between HA group 1 and group 2
plot_HA_G1_VS_G2<- mds_data %>%
                  filter(ha_group != "neg") %>% 
                  ggplot(aes(MDS1,MDS2,color = ha_group)) +
                  geom_point() +
                  stat_ellipse() +
                  theme_classic(base_size=18)


# looking at Beta diversity differences between HA group 1 and uninfected
plot_HA_G1_VS_NEG<- mds_data %>%
                  filter(ha_group != "2") %>% 
                  ggplot(aes(MDS1,MDS2,color = ha_group)) +
                  geom_point() +
                  stat_ellipse() +
                  theme_classic(base_size=18)

# looking at Beta diversity differences between HA group 2 and uninfected
plot_HA_G2_VS_NEG<- mds_data %>%
                  filter(ha_group != "1") %>% 
                  ggplot(aes(MDS1,MDS2,color = ha_group)) +
                  geom_point() +
                  stat_ellipse() +
                  theme_classic(base_size=18)

plot_HA_G1_VS_G2_VS_neg

plot_HA_G1_VS_G2

plot_HA_G1_VS_NEG

plot_HA_G2_VS_NEG

#Step 5)

Perform a PERMANOVA test to find statistical significance between the groups using an Adonis function.

metadata <- sample_data(psrare) %>% data.frame() # to make the next lines easier


set.seed(421)
# Single variable anova 

Permanova_on_HA_groups <- adonis2(ps_WUF ~ ha_group, data = metadata, permutations = 99999)

Permanova_on_HA_groups
## Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
## Permutation: free
## Number of permutations: 99999
## 
## adonis2(formula = ps_WUF ~ ha_group, data = metadata, permutations = 99999)
##           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F  Pr(>F)  
## Model      2  0.01799 0.04726 2.6789 0.02326 *
## Residual 108  0.36257 0.95274                 
## Total    110  0.38056 1.00000                 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# when observing if their is a difference in the Beta diversity between Group 1,2 or negative control statistical significance was seen p= 0.02326


#this statistical significance led to us comparing each individual group within the ha_group column: group1 vs group 2 vs uninfected


# Comparing HA G1 Vs G2 using PERMANOVA 
compare_G1_vs_G2 = c('2','1')

# Correct the data to make them have the same size when comparing G1 Vs G2
keep_G1_vs_G2 = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G1_vs_G2) %>% rownames()


ps_WUF_G1_vs_G2 = as.matrix(ps_WUF)[keep_G1_vs_G2,keep_G1_vs_G2] %>% as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA on G1 vs G2
Permanova_G1_vs_G2 = adonis2(ps_WUF_G1_vs_G2 ~ ha_group,
                       data = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G1_vs_G2), permutations = 99999)

Permanova_G1_vs_G2 
## Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
## Permutation: free
## Number of permutations: 99999
## 
## adonis2(formula = ps_WUF_G1_vs_G2 ~ ha_group, data = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G1_vs_G2), permutations = 99999)
##          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
## Model     1 0.002985 0.01671 1.1554 0.3286
## Residual 68 0.175700 0.98329              
## Total    69 0.178685 1.00000
# This one showed no significance p= 0.3286 indcation no differences between the beta diversity of G1 vs G2

# Comparing HA G1 Vs Neg using PERMANOVA 
compare_G1_vs_neg = c('neg','1')

# Correct the data to make them have the same size when comparing G1 Vs neg
keep_G1_vs_neg = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G1_vs_neg) %>% rownames()


ps_WUF_G1_vs_neg = as.matrix(ps_WUF)[keep_G1_vs_neg,keep_G1_vs_neg] %>% as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA on G1 vs neg
Permanova_G1_vs_neg = adonis2(ps_WUF_G1_vs_neg ~ ha_group,
                       data = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G1_vs_neg), permutations = 99999)

Permanova_G1_vs_neg 
## Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
## Permutation: free
## Number of permutations: 99999
## 
## adonis2(formula = ps_WUF_G1_vs_neg ~ ha_group, data = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G1_vs_neg), permutations = 99999)
##          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F  Pr(>F)  
## Model     1 0.009279 0.03227 2.4341 0.09864 .
## Residual 73 0.278293 0.96773                 
## Total    74 0.287572 1.00000                 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# This one showed no significance p= 0.09864 indcation no differences between the beta diversity of G1 vs neg


# Comparing HA G2 Vs Neg using PERMANOVA 
compare_G2_vs_neg = c('neg','2')

# Correct the data to make them have the same size when comparing G2 Vs neg
keep_G2_vs_neg = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G2_vs_neg) %>% rownames()


ps_WUF_G2_vs_neg = as.matrix(ps_WUF)[keep_G2_vs_neg,keep_G2_vs_neg] %>% as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA on G2 vs neg
Permanova_G2_vs_neg = adonis2(ps_WUF_G2_vs_neg ~ ha_group,
                       data = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G2_vs_neg), permutations = 99999)

Permanova_G2_vs_neg 
## Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
## Permutation: free
## Number of permutations: 99999
## 
## adonis2(formula = ps_WUF_G2_vs_neg ~ ha_group, data = metadata %>% filter(ha_group %in% compare_G2_vs_neg), permutations = 99999)
##          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)  
## Model     1 0.014177 0.04969 3.9213 0.0142 *
## Residual 75 0.271150 0.95031                
## Total    76 0.285326 1.00000                
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# This one showed no significance p= 0.0142 indcation a statisticaly significant differences between the beta diversity of G2 vs neg. This indicates that group 2 HA influenza viruses may have a stronger affect in changing the beta diversity of the coacle microbiome.
